headline: The post-May '68 Christian philosophy now being embraced by the American far right
description: Trump supporters often blame the cultural upheavals triggered by the May 1968 protests in France for sowing the seeds of 'wokeness.' Yet some of them are now claiming a different French school of thought, which took shape at Stanford University under the influence of René Girard.

headline: Russian bombardment of Kyiv kills four
description: The attacks came as retaliation after Ukrainian strikes in Russian-occupied eastern Ukraine killed 21 students.

headline: Roland-Garros 2026: New crop of French tennis talents emerges
description: Moïse Kouamé and Ksenia Efremova are two of France's most exciting emerging tennis talents. But at just 17 years old, they will need to learn to balance promising skills and intense scrutiny.

headline: Economist Guillaume Vallet: 'The Enhanced Games glorifies a certain type of man, dominant and celebrated by MAGA ideology'
description: In an interview, the economics professor analyzes this competition between doped athletes, scheduled for May 24 in Las Vegas. Advocating for an unregulated market logic, these unprecedented Games in the history of sport introduce a new paradigm of confrontation, outside the democratic framework.

headline: Paris left splits over awarding of city's marathon to Bolloré-linked company
description: The Cadence group – which notably includes Havas, owned by billionaire Vincent Bolloré – will organize the next four Paris marathons and half-marathons, the Paris City Council decided on Wednesday.

headline: New Ebola outbreak in the DR Congo puts international solidarity to the test
